title: Design System: Concentration du patrimoine en France

display.purpose: Page and view titles. Source Serif 4, one per screen.

headline.purpose: Section headings and chart titles. Source Serif 4.

title.purpose: Figure headings, filter-group labels, table captions. Source Sans 3.

body.purpose: Explanatory prose. Source Sans 3, measure capped 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Filter labels, buttons, axis ticks, the traceability line. Source Sans 3, often muted ink.

data.purpose: Numerals in axes, tables, tooltips, inline figures. Source Sans 3 with tabular figures.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Transient overlays only (dropdown, popover, chart tooltip). The only shadow in the system; never on resting surfaces.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions. No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: State changes: filter toggles, tab switches.

motion.name: duration-chart
motion.purpose: Chart redraw on filter change. Disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary actions only (apply filters, export). Slate-teal fill, rare on screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.55 0.075 220); color: oklch(0.985 0.006 85); font-family: "Source Sans 3", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 10px 18px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.46 0.085 222); } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: oklch(0.40 0.078 221); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.075 220); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: oklch(0.96 0.008 83); color: oklch(0.52 0.013 74); cursor: not-allowed; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-primary { transition: none; } }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default for most actions. Panel fill with a hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: oklch(0.96 0.008 83); color: oklch(0.26 0.015 72); font-family: "Source Sans 3", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 10px 18px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.89 0.009 80); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: oklch(0.93 0.010 82); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.075 220); outline-offset: 2px; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-secondary { transition: none; } }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: First-level filters (Convention: unité/concept, source toggles). Default and active state shown; active uses the slate-teal wash.
components.css: .ds-chip { background: oklch(0.96 0.008 83); color: oklch(0.26 0.015 72); font-family: "Source Sans 3", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 6px 12px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.89 0.009 80); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-chip:hover { background: oklch(0.93 0.010 82); } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.075 220); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-chip--active { background: oklch(0.93 0.03 218); color: oklch(0.46 0.085 222); border-color: oklch(0.55 0.075 220); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-chip { transition: none; } }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Text field. Paper fill, hairline stroke, accent ring on focus, no glow.
components.css: .ds-field { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: oklch(0.985 0.006 85); border: 1px solid oklch(0.89 0.009 80); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-field:focus-within { border-color: oklch(0.55 0.075 220); outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.075 220); outline-offset: 1px; } .ds-input { border: none; background: transparent; outline: none; font-family: "Source Sans 3", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: oklch(0.26 0.015 72); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.52 0.013 74); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-field { transition: none; } }

components.name: Tab Navigation
components.description: Top-level views. Text tabs, muted ink inactive, ink with a slate-teal underline when active. No pills, no boxes.
components.html: Tableau de bordComparaison de sourcesSources & méthodologie
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; gap: 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(0.89 0.009 80); } .ds-tab { font-family: "Source Sans 3", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; color: oklch(0.52 0.013 74); padding: 8px 0; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; margin-bottom: -1px; cursor: pointer; text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-tab:hover { color: oklch(0.26 0.015 72); } .ds-tab--active { color: oklch(0.26 0.015 72); border-bottom-color: oklch(0.55 0.075 220); } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.075 220); outline-offset: 2px; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-tab { transition: none; } }

components.name: Series Legend Line
components.description: Signature pattern. Each plotted series is identified by an Okabe-Ito swatch, the source name, and its Convention (unité/concept). Conventions differing means dash patterns differing, so overlaid sources never read as merged.
components.html:
• WIDadulte / net
  • INSEEmenage / brut
components.css: .ds-legend { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-legend__item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: "Source Sans 3", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-legend__name { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.26 0.015 72); } .ds-legend__conv { font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: oklch(0.52 0.013 74); }

narrative.overview: A calm, well-lit public reading room where anyone can come and read the record, and trust it. The interface is the quiet room; the data is what's on the table. On a politically charged subject, neutrality is conveyed by restraint: the chrome recedes, the figures and their provenance carry the page. This is data journalism in the lineage of Our World in Data, an explainer with the rigor of a statistical instrument underneath. It is not a dashboard and it is not an op-ed.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-paper surface (oklch(0.985 0.006 85)); neutral ink; chrome that recedes.
One muted slate-teal accent (oklch(0.55 0.075 220)), <=10% of any screen, for interaction only.
Saturated color reserved for plotted data; colorblind-safe series plus non-hue cues.
Source Serif 4 display over Source Sans 3 body; tabular figures for all data.
Flat by default; motion is responsive feedback (150-220ms), never choreography.

rules.name: The Quiet Chrome Rule
rules.body: Color belongs to the data. The interface chrome stays near-neutral; the only chromatic UI element is the slate-teal accent, and it appears on <=10% of any screen. If the shell competes with the chart for attention, the shell is too loud.

rules.name: The Impartial Hue Rule
rules.body: No politically-coded color carries meaning in the UI. The primary accent is never red or partisan blue; series colors encode source identity, never a value judgment. Hue is information, never editorial.

rules.name: The Tabular Figures Rule
rules.body: Every numeral in a data context (axes, tables, tooltips, inline figures) uses tabular figures (font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1) so columns align and values don't jitter as they update. Proportional figures are for prose only.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadow appears only as a transient response to state. If a resting element needs a shadow to be understood, fix the layout before reaching for elevation.

dos: Do keep chrome near-neutral and spend saturated color only on plotted data (The Quiet Chrome Rule).
Do label every plotted line with its Convention (unité/concept) and reinforce series with non-hue cues (direct labels, dash patterns), so meaning survives colorblindness and grayscale.
Do keep source, millesime_source, and date_extraction visible for every displayed figure. Traceability is a feature, not a footnote.
Do annotate the 2018 ISF->IFI rupture and millésime révisions calmly with the muted-amber marker. Explain the break; don't smooth it.
Do use tabular figures for all numeric data, fixed-rem type sizes, and a body measure capped at 65-75ch.
Do keep surfaces flat and warm; tint every neutral and never use #000 or #fff.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no KPI hero-metric cards, no gradient accents, no identical icon-heading-text card grids, no analytics-template chrome.
Don't go crypto / fintech dark-neon: no dark-mode-by-default, no neon-on-black, no glowing charts.
Don't settle for government-portal drab: no flat, cramped, low-contrast administrative look. Civic credibility comes from craft, not dullness.
Don't make a flashy infographic: no decorative or animation-heavy charts that bend the data for effect.
Don't make a red or partisan-blue color carry meaning in the UI, and don't use alarm-red (#dc2626) for the 2018 rupture; it's a methodological seam, not a warning (The Impartial Hue Rule).
Don't encode anything by color alone, and don't merge series from different Conventions into one quantity. Overlay with explicit labels only.
